svg.text: • Boyer-Moore: long patterns, large alphabets (English text, source code, binaries).
Sublinear average — skips m characters per shift on natural text.
• KMP: short patterns, small alphabets (DNA over {A,C,G,T}), streaming input that
cannot back up. Deterministic linear, no large preprocessing tables.
• Rabin-Karp: multi-pattern same-length set membership, 2D matching, fingerprint-based.
• Aho-Corasick: many short patterns, single text pass — multi-pattern KMP generalization.
Real grep/ripgrep use Two-Way (Crochemore-Perrin) or Boyer-Moore-Horspool for short needles,
SIMD memchr for the bad-character step, then verify with the rest.
• Boyer-Moore-Horspool: bad-character only, simpler, slightly worse worst case.
Used in libc memmem on many platforms.
• Sunday's algorithm: looks one char past the alignment — often even faster.
• Two-Way (Crochemore-Perrin): O(1) extra space, O(n) worst case, used in glibc.
• Turbo-BM: remembers prior matched factor — guarantees 2n-1 comparisons.
Modern engines: SIMD scan for first char, then BM-Horspool style verify.
Key insight: hardware memchr beats algorithmic skips for short patterns.
